base_case: Cepeda v. Cumberland Engineering Co., 76 N.J. 152 (1978)
constructor: Venkateshwar Reddy Jambula (drafted with Claude Opus as programming and writing assistant)

perturbations.original_text: The usefulness and desirability of the pelletizing machine to industrial users — essential equipment for plastic processing, with substantial commercial utility.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (product utility) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Auto-generated template rewrite for factor f1 (product_utility). Human review recommended before final dataset admission.

perturbations.original_text: The likelihood of operator injury during foreseeable use of the pelletizing machine, given the physical design of the intake port and the nature of factory work rhythms.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (probability of harm) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Auto-generated template rewrite for factor f2 (probability_of_harm). Human review recommended before final dataset admission.

perturbations.original_text: The probable seriousness of injury from the pelletizing machine's intake rollers — finger amputation, hand injury, permanent disability.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (gravity of loss) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Auto-generated template rewrite for factor f3 (gravity_of_loss). Human review recommended before final dataset admission.

perturbations.original_text: The availability of a safer alternative design — specifically an interlock guard that physically prevents hand insertion into the intake, or a presence-detection sensor that stops the rollers when a hand approaches.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (alternative precautions) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.

perturbations.original_text: The cost and feasibility of incorporating an interlock guard into the pelletizing machine design — manufacturing cost, impact on throughput, maintenance burden.

perturbations.original_text: Factory worker's anticipated awareness of the point-of-operation hazard — whether the risk is obvious to an ordinary operator or concealed by the machine's design.

perturbations.original_text: Feasibility of spreading the loss via product price increases and manufacturer liability insurance, as compared with placing the loss on the injured worker.
